title: My Plant by the Window
summary: A narrator in Berlin describes the spider plant on her kitchen window and the simple care it needs.
body: I have a green plant by the kitchen window. Its name is a spider plant. The leaves are long and thin. They are green with a white line in the middle. My plant came from my flatmate Anna. Anna moved to another city last year. She gave me her plant. She said, 'Please take care of it. It is easy.' And she was right. I water the plant once a week, on Sunday. I give it a little water, not too much. I clean the leaves with a soft cloth. The window is sunny in the morning. The plant likes the sun. It does not like cold air. In winter I close the window early. Last month I saw something new. A small baby plant came out from the side. It hangs down on a thin stem. Now there are three baby plants. I can give one to my new flatmate. He just moved in last week. A plant is a good first gift in a new home.

annotations.meaningVi: cây dây nhện (cây cảnh phổ biến)

annotations.exampleEn: The leaves are long and green.

annotations.meaningVi: bạn cùng nhà / cùng căn hộ

annotations.exampleEn: Please take care of my cat.
I see my friend once a week.
Wipe the screen with a soft cloth.
The plant does not like cold air.
A baby plant grew from the side.
It hangs on a long stem.
My new neighbour moved in yesterday.

grammarNotes.title: Frequency: once a week, every day, in winter
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Có nhiều cách nói tần suất ở A1: 'once a week' (một tuần một lần), 'every day' (hàng ngày), 'in winter' (vào mùa đông). Các cụm này thường đứng cuối câu hoặc đầu câu: 'I water the plant once a week, on Sunday.' / 'In winter I close the window early.'
grammarNotes.bodyEn: A1 frequency expressions include 'once a week', 'every day', 'in winter'. They go at the end or start of a sentence: 'I water the plant once a week, on Sunday.' / 'In winter I close the window early.'

grammarNotes.title: 'Last week / last month / last year' for finished time
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Khi nói về một thời điểm đã xong, ta dùng 'last + thời gian' + động từ quá khứ: 'Anna moved to another city last year.' / 'Last month I saw something new.' / 'He just moved in last week.' Không có 'in/on/at' trước 'last'.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'last + time' with simple past for a finished moment: 'Anna moved to another city last year.' / 'Last month I saw something new.' / 'He just moved in last week.' No preposition before 'last'.

translationVi: Tôi có một cây xanh đặt bên cửa sổ nhà bếp. Tên nó là cây dây nhện. Lá dài và mảnh. Lá màu xanh với một đường trắng ở giữa. Cây của tôi là của Anna, bạn cùng nhà cũ. Anna chuyển sang một thành phố khác vào năm ngoái. Bạn ấy tặng tôi cây của mình. Bạn ấy nói: 'Cậu chăm cây giúp tớ nhé. Dễ lắm.' Và bạn ấy nói đúng. Mỗi tuần tôi tưới cây một lần, vào chủ nhật. Tôi tưới một ít, không quá nhiều. Tôi lau lá bằng vải mềm. Cửa sổ buổi sáng có nắng. Cây thích nắng. Nó không thích không khí lạnh. Mùa đông tôi đóng cửa sổ sớm. Tháng trước tôi thấy điều mới. Một cây con nhỏ mọc ra từ bên hông. Nó treo lủng lẳng trên một cuống mảnh. Giờ đã có ba cây con. Tôi có thể tặng một cây cho bạn cùng nhà mới. Anh ấy vừa chuyển đến tuần trước. Một cái cây là món quà đầu tiên tốt khi vào nhà mới.

questions.explanation: 'I have a green plant by the kitchen window.'

questions.prompt: What kind of plant is it?
What do the leaves look like?
options: Long and thin, green with a white line
questions.explanation: 'They are green with a white line in the middle.'
'My plant came from my flatmate Anna.'
'Anna moved to another city last year.'

questions.prompt: How often does the narrator water the plant?
questions.explanation: 'I water the plant once a week, on Sunday.'

questions.prompt: What does she use to clean the leaves?
questions.explanation: 'I clean the leaves with a soft cloth.'

questions.prompt: What does the plant not like?
What did the narrator do in winter?
options: Moved the plant to the bathroom
questions.explanation: 'In winter I close the window early.'

questions.prompt: What did she see last month?
options: A small baby plant grew on the side
questions.explanation: 'A small baby plant came out from the side.'

questions.prompt: How many baby plants are there now?
What is the main idea of the last sentences?
options: She wants to sell the baby plants
A plant can be a kind welcome gift for a new flatmate
questions.explanation: 'A plant is a good first gift in a new home.'

cloze.template: I have a green plant by the kitchen {{b1}}. Its name is a {{b2}} plant. The leaves are long and thin. They are green with a white {{b3}} in the middle. My plant came from my {{b4}} Anna. Anna moved to another city last {{b5}}. She gave me her plant. She said, 'Please take care of it. It is easy.' I water the plant {{b6}} a week, on Sunday. I give it a little water, not too much. I clean the leaves with a soft {{b7}}. The window is sunny in the morning. The plant likes the {{b8}}. It does not like cold air. In winter I close the window {{b9}}. Last month I saw something new. A small {{b10}} plant came out from the side. Now there are {{b11}} baby plants. I can give one to my new flatmate. He just moved in last week. A plant is a good first {{b12}} in a new home.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why does a small plant make a new home feel less empty? What other simple gift can welcome a new neighbour or flatmate?
